# Dawn System — Copilot Instructions

## Project Overview
`dawn-system` is a **Foundry VTT V14** game system written in TypeScript.
It is compiled with **Rollup** (`rollup -c`) and outputs a single ESM bundle to `dist/dawn-system.mjs`, which Foundry loads via the `esmodules` field in `system.json`.

## Repository Layout
```
dawn-system/
  src/
    dawn-system.ts          # Entry point — Hooks.once("init", …)
    data/
      actor-models.ts       # CharacterDataModel, AdversaryDataModel
  dist/                     # Compiled output (git-ignored, regenerated by build)
  lang/en.json              # Localisation strings
  styles/dawn-system.css    # Stylesheet
  system.json               # Foundry package manifest
  tsconfig.json
  rollup.config.mjs
  package.json
```

## Build & Workflow
```bash
npm install          # Install dev dependencies
npm run build        # One-shot compile → dist/dawn-system.mjs
npm run watch        # Recompile on save
npm run typecheck    # Type-check only (uses tsconfig.json with Foundry types)
```

The system is symlinked into the Foundry user-data directory:
`~/Apps/V14/foundry-data/Data/systems/dawn-system → /home/evelyn/src/dawn-system`

## Dual tsconfig Design
Two tsconfig files are used to work around `@rollup/plugin-typescript`'s
behaviour of compiling every JS file it discovers via `allowJs`:

| File | Purpose | Has Foundry types? | Emits? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tsconfig.json` | IDE + `npm run typecheck` | ✅ via `src/global.d.ts` | No (`noEmit`) |
| `tsconfig.build.json` | Rollup build only | ❌ (just strips TS syntax) | Yes → `dist/` |

Rollup uses `tsconfig.build.json` which excludes `src/global.d.ts` to avoid
pulling in the entire Foundry source tree as compile targets (TS5055 errors).

## Foundry VTT V14 Key Concepts

### Globals (available at runtime without importing)
All Foundry APIs are injected as globals. Do **not** import them; just use them.
In V14 most classes moved into the `foundry.*` namespace:

| V13 (deprecated) | V14 (correct) | TypeScript typed? |
|---|---|---|
| `Hooks.once(…)` | `foundry.helpers.Hooks.once(…)` | ✅ |
| `Actor` | `foundry.documents.Actor` | ✅ |
| `Item` | `foundry.documents.Item` | ✅ |
| `CONFIG` | `CONFIG` (unchanged) | ✅ |
| `foundry.abstract.TypeDataModel` | (unchanged) | ✅ |
| `foundry.data.fields.*` | (unchanged) | ✅ |

### Adding a New Actor Type
1. Add the type key to `"documentTypes".Actor` in `system.json` (no value needed: `"mytype": {}`).
2. Create a `TypeDataModel` subclass in `src/data/actor-models.ts`.
3. Register it in `src/dawn-system.ts` inside the `init` hook:
   ```ts
   CONFIG.Actor.dataModels.mytype = MyTypeDataModel;
   ```
4. Add localisation to `lang/en.json` under `TYPES.Actor`.

### Adding a New Item Type
Same pattern — use `CONFIG.Item.dataModels` and `"documentTypes".Item` in `system.json`.

### Data Fields Reference
```ts
const { NumberField, StringField, SchemaField, HTMLField, BooleanField, ArrayField, ObjectField } =
  foundry.data.fields;
```

`NumberField({ required, integer, min, max, initial, label })`
`StringField({ required, blank, initial, label })`
`SchemaField({ fieldName: new SomeField(…) })`

### Token Attributes
Register which system fields appear as Token bars/values:
```ts
CONFIG.Actor.trackableAttributes = {
  mytype: {
    bar: ["resources.health"],   // SchemaFields with value+max
    value: ["level"],            // Plain numeric fields
  },
};
```

### Custom Actor/Item Implementations
Extend `Actor` / `Item`, then set `CONFIG.Actor.documentClass = MyActor`.

## TypeScript Setup
- Types come from the **Foundry V14 source** at `~/Apps/V14/foundry/`:
  - Global declarations: `client/global.d.mts`, `common/global.d.mts`
  - Data fields: `common/data/fields.mjs`
  - TypeDataModel: `common/abstract/type-data.mjs`
  - Actor document: `client/documents/actor.mjs`
  - Hooks: `client/helpers/hooks.mjs` (V14: `foundry.helpers.Hooks`)
  - CONFIG: `client/config.mjs`
- `tsconfig.json` maps `@common/*` → `~/Apps/V14/foundry/common/*` and `@client/*` → `~/Apps/V14/foundry/client/*`.
- `src/global.d.ts` uses `/// <reference path>` directives to pull in `client/global.d.mts` and `common/global.d.mts`.
- `skipLibCheck: true` and `checkJs: false` suppress noise from non-TS Foundry source files.
- Because Foundry APIs are globals, TypeScript picks them up from the `global.d.mts` files; **no imports needed** in system code.
- **Type casting patterns**: Foundry's JSDoc types are sometimes incomplete. Use casts where needed:
  ```ts
  // dataModels expects typeof TypeDataModel but subclasses trigger generic variance errors
  (CONFIG.Actor.dataModels as Record<string, unknown>).myType = MyModel;
  // trackableAttributes is typed as Record<string, string> but runtime expects {bar, value}
  const trackable = CONFIG.Actor as unknown as {
    trackableAttributes: Record<string, { bar: string[]; value: string[] }>;
  };
  ```

## Documentation Links
- System Development Guide: https://foundryvtt.com/article/system-development/
- System Data Models: https://foundryvtt.com/article/system-data-models/
- API Reference: https://foundryvtt.com/api/
- BaseSystem manifest fields: https://foundryvtt.com/api/classes/foundry.packages.BaseSystem.html
- D&D5e reference system (open source): https://github.com/foundryvtt/dnd5e

## Conventions
- All source files use `*.ts` with strict TypeScript.
- Foundry globals are used directly without imports.
- Local module imports use `.js` extensions (resolved to `.ts` at build time by rollup/tsc).
- Localisation keys follow `DAWN.Document.Type.Field` for system strings and `TYPES.Document.type` for type labels.
- `system.json` version follows semver; bump on every release.
